title: Zymoclean Gel DNA Recovery Protocol
author: Zymo Research
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.gitbuen

steps.title: Excise the DNA fragment 1 from...
steps.instruction: Excise the DNA fragment 1 from the agarose gel using a razor blade, scalpel or other device and transfer it into a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. 1 The amount of agarose excised from the gel should be as small as possible.

steps.title: Add 3 volumes of ADB to...
steps.instruction: Add 3 volumes of ADB to each volume of agarose excised from the gel (e.g. for 100 μl (mg) of agarose gel slice add 300 μl of ADB ).

steps.title: Incubate at 37-55 °C for 5-10...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37-55 °C for 5-10 minutes until the gel slice is completely dissolved 2 . 00:10:00 For DNA fragments > 8 kb, following the incubation step, add one additional volume (equal to that of the gel slice) of water to the mixture for better DNA recovery ( e.g., 100 μl agarose, 300 μl ADB , and 100 μl water). For DNA fragments > 8 kb, following the incubation step, add one additional volume (equal to that of the gel slice) of water to the mixture for better DNA recovery (e.g., 100 μl agarose, 300 μl ADB, and 100 μl water). For DNA fragments > 8 kb, following the incubation step, add one additional volume (equal to that of the gel slice) of water to the mixture for better DNA recovery ( e.g., 100 μl agarose, 300 μl ADB , and 100 μl water). 2 Do not incubate above 60°C. It is important that the gel slice dissolve completely. This can be facilitated by gentle mixing during the incubation.

steps.title: Transfer the melted agarose solution to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the melted agarose solution to a Zymo-Spin™ Column in a Collection Tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 30-60 seconds
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 30-60 seconds. Discard the flow-through 3 . 00:01:00 3 Remove the flow-through by aspiration. Avoid contamination of the collection tube rim. All centrifugation steps should be performed between 10,000 - 16,000 x g.

steps.title: Add 200 μl of DNA Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of DNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 30 seconds. Discard the flow-through. (wash 1/2) 00:00:30

steps.title: Add 200 μl of DNA Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of DNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 30 seconds. Discard the flow-through. (wash 2/2) 00:00:30

steps.title: Add ≥ 6 μl DNA Elution...
steps.instruction: Add ≥ 6 μl DNA Elution Buffer 4 or water 5 directly to the column matrix. 4 DNA Elution Buffer: 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.5, 0.1 mM EDTA. 5 Elution of DNA from the column is dependent on pH and temperature. If water is used, make sure the pH is >6.0. Waiting 1 minute prior to elution may improve the yield of larger (> 6 kb) DNA. For even larger DNA (> 10 kb), the total yield may be improved by eluting the DNA with 60-70 ºC DNA Elution Buffer.

steps.title: Place column into a 1
steps.instruction: Place column into a 1.5 ml tube and centrifuge for 30-60 seconds to elute DNA. 00:01:00 All centrifugation steps should be performed between 10,000 - 16,000 x g.

steps.title: Ultra-pure DNA is now ready for use
steps.instruction: Ultra-pure DNA is now ready for use.
